Overview

Address
190 NE 191ST STREET, MIAMI, FL 33161
Phone
3056519690
Certified beds
120
Avg daily residents
116 (96% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1992-03-11
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.55 3.18 4.31 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.58 0.56 0.71 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.58 0.71 0.70 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.39 1.92 2.90 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.16 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.40
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.50
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.12
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
29.5%
annual
RN turnover
43.5%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.13
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.82
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-08-30 38 31 13 536 2 804
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-05-19 12 5 8 131 1 131
Total weighted health score
635.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (60)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

55 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
4 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-06-27 2025-07-31
0627 Ensure the transfer/discharge meets the resident's needs/preferences and that the resident is prepared for a safe transfer/discharge. D 2025-06-27 2025-07-31
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-06-27 2025-07-31
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. D 2025-06-27 2025-07-31
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-03-27 2025-05-10
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-03-27 2025-05-10
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-03-27 2025-05-10
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0576 Ensure residents have reasonable access to and privacy in their use of communication methods. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. G 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0800 Provide each resident with a nourishing, palatable, well-balanced diet that meets his or her daily nutritional and special dietary needs. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0808 Ensure therapeutic diets are prescribed by the attending physician and may be delegated to a registered or licensed dietitian, to the extent allowed by State law. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0810 Provide special eating equipment and utensils for residents who need them and appropriate assistance. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. K 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. F 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0917 Make sure each resident has 1) at least one window to the outside in a room; 2) a room at or above ground level; 3) adequate bedding; 4) furniture that meets the resident's needs; or 5) adequate closet space. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. K 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. K 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0924 Put firmly secured handrails on each side of hallways. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-28
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2024-08-01 2024-08-31
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2023-10-03 2024-02-02
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. E 2023-10-03 2024-02-02
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-05-19 2023-06-23
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2023-05-19 2023-06-23
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-05-19 2023-06-23
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. D 2023-05-19 2023-06-23
0839 Employ staff that are licensed, certified, or registered in accordance with state laws. D 2023-05-19 2023-06-23

Deficiencies 0803 — description Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. 0803.description
